# Swap.Htmx - Server-Driven State Management for ASP.NET + HTMX

> This document describes SwapState, the state management pattern in Swap.Htmx.
> Swap.Htmx is a .NET library that makes HTMX a first-class citizen in ASP.NET MVC.

## Core Principle: HTML Is The State

SwapState uses hidden HTML fields to persist state across HTMX requests. The server renders state, the browser sends it back, and the server renders updated state. No JavaScript state management required.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Server renders HTML with hidden fields                       │
│     <input type="hidden" name="Category" value="Electronics" /> │
│                                                                  │
│  2. User interacts (clicks button, types in input)              │
│     hx-include="#state-container" sends hidden fields           │
│                                                                  │
│  3. Server receives state via [FromSwapState]                   │
│     Controller action gets populated state object               │
│                                                                  │
│  4. Server returns new HTML with updated state                  │
│     Hidden fields reflect new values                            │
│                                                                  │
│  5. Repeat                                                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Start

### Step 1: Define a State Class

Create a class that inherits from `SwapState`:

```csharp
public class ProductFilterState : SwapState
{
    public string Category { get; set; } = "all";
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    public int PageSize { get; set; } = 10;
    public string? Search { get; set; }
    public string SortBy { get; set; } = "name";
    public bool SortDesc { get; set; } = false;
    public bool InStockOnly { get; set; } = false;
}
```

The `SwapState` base class provides:
- `ContainerId`: Auto-generated ID based on class name (e.g., `product-filter-state`)
- Change tracking (optional)
- URL sync capability (optional)

### Step 2: Render State in Your View

Use the `<swap-state>` tag helper to render hidden fields:

```html
@model ProductViewModel

<!-- Renders hidden inputs for ALL state properties -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Example output: -->
<!-- <div id="product-filter-state" class="swap-state-container"> -->
<!--     <input type="hidden" name="Category" value="all" /> -->
<!--     <input type="hidden" name="Page" value="1" /> -->
<!--     <input type="hidden" name="PageSize" value="10" /> -->
<!--     <input type="hidden" name="Search" value="" /> -->
<!--     ... -->
<!-- </div> -->
```

### Step 3: Include State in HTMX Requests

Use `hx-include` to send state with requests:

```html
<!-- Button that changes category -->
<button hx-get="/Home/Filter?Category=Electronics&Page=1"
        hx-target="#content"
        hx-include="#product-filter-state">
    Electronics
</button>

<!-- Search input that sends on keyup -->
<input type="text" 
       name="Search"
       value="@Model.State.Search"
       hx-get="/Home/Filter?Page=1"
       hx-target="#content"
       hx-include="#product-filter-state"
       hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms" />
```

### Step 4: Bind State in Controller

Use `[FromSwapState]` to bind the state:

```csharp
public class HomeController : Controller
{
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        var state = new ProductFilterState();
        return View(new ProductViewModel { State = state, Products = GetProducts(state) });
    }

    [HttpGet]
    public IActionResult Filter([FromSwapState] ProductFilterState state)
    {
        // state is fully populated from hidden fields
        var products = GetProducts(state);
        
        // Return partial with updated state
        return PartialView("_ProductList", new ProductViewModel 
        { 
            State = state, 
            Products = products 
        });
    }
}
```

## Complete Example: Product Filter

### Model

```csharp
public class ProductFilterState : SwapState
{
    public string Category { get; set; } = "all";
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    public string? Search { get; set; }
    public bool InStockOnly { get; set; } = false;
}

public class ProductViewModel
{
    public ProductFilterState State { get; set; } = new();
    public List<Product> Products { get; set; } = new();
    public int TotalCount { get; set; }
}
```

### Controller

```csharp
public class ProductController : Controller
{
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        var state = new ProductFilterState();
        var (products, total) = FilterProducts(state);
        return View(new ProductViewModel { State = state, Products = products, TotalCount = total });
    }

    [HttpGet]
    public IActionResult Filter([FromSwapState] ProductFilterState state)
    {
        var (products, total) = FilterProducts(state);
        return PartialView("_ProductList", new ProductViewModel 
        { 
            State = state, 
            Products = products, 
            TotalCount = total 
        });
    }

    private (List<Product>, int) FilterProducts(ProductFilterState state)
    {
        var query = _products.AsQueryable();
        
        if (state.Category != "all")
            query = query.Where(p => p.Category == state.Category);
        
        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(state.Search))
            query = query.Where(p => p.Name.Contains(state.Search, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
        
        if (state.InStockOnly)
            query = query.Where(p => p.InStock);
        
        var total = query.Count();
        var products = query.Skip((state.Page - 1) * 10).Take(10).ToList();
        
        return (products, total);
    }
}
```

### Main View (Index.cshtml)

```html
@model ProductViewModel
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Products</title>
    <script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2.0.4"></script>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Products</h1>
    
    <!-- Content area that gets swapped -->
    <div id="content">
        @await Html.PartialAsync("_ProductList", Model)
    </div>
</body>
</html>
```

### Partial View (_ProductList.cshtml)

```html
@model ProductViewModel
@{
    var state = Model.State;
}

<!-- STATE CONTAINER - hidden fields persist state -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Category Filter -->
<div class="filters">
    @foreach (var cat in new[] { "all", "Electronics", "Furniture", "Office" })
    {
        var isActive = state.Category == cat ? "active" : "";
        <button class="@isActive"
                hx-get="/Product/Filter?Category=@cat&Page=1"
                hx-target="#content"
                hx-include="#@state.ContainerId">
            @cat
        </button>
    }
</div>

<!-- Search -->
<input type="text" 
       name="Search"
       placeholder="Search..."
       value="@state.Search"
       hx-get="/Product/Filter?Page=1"
       hx-target="#content"
       hx-include="#@state.ContainerId"
       hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms" />

<!-- In Stock Checkbox -->
<label>
    <input type="checkbox" 
           @(state.InStockOnly ? "checked" : "")
           hx-get="/Product/Filter?Page=1&InStockOnly=@(!state.InStockOnly ? "true" : "false")"
           hx-target="#content"
           hx-include="#@state.ContainerId" />
    In Stock Only
</label>

<!-- Product List -->
<table>
    @foreach (var product in Model.Products)
    {
        <tr>
            <td>@product.Name</td>
            <td>@product.Category</td>
            <td>@product.Price</td>
        </tr>
    }
</table>

<!-- Pagination -->
<div class="pagination">
    @for (int i = 1; i <= Math.Ceiling(Model.TotalCount / 10.0); i++)
    {
        var pageNum = i;
        <button hx-get="/Product/Filter?Page=@pageNum"
                hx-target="#content"
                hx-include="#@state.ContainerId"
                class="@(state.Page == pageNum ? "active" : "")">
            @pageNum
        </button>
    }
</div>
```

## Complete Example: Multi-Step Wizard

SwapState excels at multi-step forms where data must persist across steps.

### Model

```csharp
public class WizardState : SwapState
{
    public int Step { get; set; } = 1;
    
    // Step 1: Personal
    public string FirstName { get; set; } = "";
    public string LastName { get; set; } = "";
    public string Email { get; set; } = "";
    
    // Step 2: Address
    public string Street { get; set; } = "";
    public string City { get; set; } = "";
    public string PostalCode { get; set; } = "";
    
    // Step 3: Preferences
    public string ContactMethod { get; set; } = "email";
    public bool Newsletter { get; set; } = false;
}

public class WizardViewModel
{
    public WizardState State { get; set; } = new();
}
```

### Controller

```csharp
public class WizardController : Controller
{
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        return View(new WizardViewModel());
    }

    [HttpPost]
    public IActionResult Step([FromSwapState] WizardState state, int goToStep)
    {
        state.Step = goToStep;
        return PartialView("_WizardContent", new WizardViewModel { State = state });
    }
    
    [HttpPost]
    public IActionResult Submit([FromSwapState] WizardState state)
    {
        // All wizard data is available in state
        // Save to database, send email, etc.
        return PartialView("_Success", state);
    }
}
```

### Wizard Partial (_WizardContent.cshtml)

```html
@model WizardViewModel
@{
    var s = Model.State;
}

<!-- STATE CONTAINER - persists ALL wizard data across steps -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Progress Indicator -->
<div class="progress">
    @for (int i = 1; i <= 3; i++)
    {
        var status = s.Step == i ? "active" : (s.Step > i ? "completed" : "");
        <span class="@status">Step @i</span>
    }
</div>

<!-- Step Content -->
<div class="step-content">
    @switch (s.Step)
    {
        case 1:
            <h3>Personal Information</h3>
            <input type="text" name="FirstName" value="@s.FirstName" placeholder="First Name" />
            <input type="text" name="LastName" value="@s.LastName" placeholder="Last Name" />
            <input type="email" name="Email" value="@s.Email" placeholder="Email" />
            break;
            
        case 2:
            <h3>Address</h3>
            <input type="text" name="Street" value="@s.Street" placeholder="Street" />
            <input type="text" name="City" value="@s.City" placeholder="City" />
            <input type="text" name="PostalCode" value="@s.PostalCode" placeholder="Postal Code" />
            break;
            
        case 3:
            <h3>Review</h3>
            <p><strong>Name:</strong> @s.FirstName @s.LastName</p>
            <p><strong>Email:</strong> @s.Email</p>
            <p><strong>Address:</strong> @s.Street, @s.City @s.PostalCode</p>
            break;
    }
</div>

<!-- Navigation -->
<div class="buttons">
    @if (s.Step > 1)
    {
        <button hx-post="/Wizard/Step?goToStep=@(s.Step - 1)"
                hx-target="#wizard-content"
                hx-include="#@s.ContainerId, .step-content input, .step-content select">
            ← Back
        </button>
    }
    
    @if (s.Step < 3)
    {
        <button hx-post="/Wizard/Step?goToStep=@(s.Step + 1)"
                hx-target="#wizard-content"
                hx-include="#@s.ContainerId, .step-content input, .step-content select">
            Next →
        </button>
    }
    else
    {
        <button hx-post="/Wizard/Submit"
                hx-target="#wizard-content"
                hx-include="#@s.ContainerId">
            Submit
        </button>
    }
</div>
```

## Key Patterns

### Pattern 1: Inputs Override Hidden Fields

When a visible input has the same `name` as a hidden field in SwapState, the visible input's value wins. This is automatic - the model binder uses the last value when duplicates exist.

```html
<!-- Hidden field from swap-state -->
<input type="hidden" name="Search" value="old value" />

<!-- Visible input (user types here) -->
<input type="text" name="Search" value="new value" />

<!-- Server receives: Search = "new value" -->
```

### Pattern 2: Checkbox Boolean Toggle

Unchecked checkboxes send nothing. To toggle a boolean, pass the opposite value explicitly:

```html
<!-- CORRECT: Explicit toggle value in URL -->
<input type="checkbox" 
       @(state.InStockOnly ? "checked" : "")
       hx-get="/Filter?InStockOnly=@(!state.InStockOnly ? "true" : "false")"
       hx-include="#@state.ContainerId" />

<!-- WRONG: Checkbox value won't uncheck properly -->
<input type="checkbox" name="InStockOnly" value="true" ... />
```

### Pattern 3: Include Form Inputs for Wizards

For multi-step forms, include both the state container AND visible form inputs:

```html
<button hx-post="/Wizard/NextStep"
        hx-target="#wizard-content"
        hx-include="#@s.ContainerId, .step-content input, .step-content select">
    Next
</button>
```

The state container has hidden fields for ALL steps (including previous steps).
The visible inputs are for the CURRENT step only.
Both get sent, and the model binder merges them (visible input values override hidden field values for current step fields).

### Pattern 4: URL Parameters Override Everything

Values in the URL override both hidden fields and form inputs:

```html
<!-- Category in URL overrides any hidden field value -->
<button hx-get="/Filter?Category=Electronics&Page=1"
        hx-include="#@state.ContainerId">
    Electronics
</button>
```

## SwapState API Reference

### SwapState Base Class

```csharp
public abstract class SwapState
{
    // Auto-generated container ID (e.g., "product-filter-state")
    public string ContainerId { get; }
    
    // Change tracking
    public HashSet<string> ChangedProperties { get; }
    public bool HasChanges { get; }
    public void AcceptChanges();
    
    // URL synchronization
    public virtual bool UrlSync => false;
    public void FromQueryString(IQueryCollection query);
    public string ToQueryString();
}
```

### [FromSwapState] Attribute

Binds a SwapState-derived class from form/query data:

```csharp
public IActionResult Filter([FromSwapState] ProductFilterState state)
{
    // state is populated from hidden fields sent via hx-include
}
```

### <swap-state> Tag Helper

Renders a state object as hidden fields:

```html
<!-- Basic usage -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- With custom container (rare) -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" include-container="false" />
```

Output:
```html
<div id="product-filter-state" class="swap-state-container">
    <input type="hidden" name="Category" value="all" />
    <input type="hidden" name="Page" value="1" />
    <!-- ... all properties ... -->
</div>
```

## When NOT to Use SwapState

1. **Sensitive data**: Don't put passwords, tokens, or secrets in SwapState. Hidden fields are visible in browser devtools.

2. **Large datasets**: SwapState adds hidden fields to HTML. Don't store hundreds of items.

3. **Server-side only state**: If state never needs to be sent back from browser, use session or database instead.

## Validation

Use standard ASP.NET validation attributes on SwapState properties:

```csharp
public class ProductFilterState : SwapState
{
    [Range(1, 1000)]
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    
    [StringLength(100)]
    public string? Search { get; set; }
    
    [RegularExpression("^(all|Electronics|Furniture|Office)$")]
    public string Category { get; set; } = "all";
}
```

Validate in controller:

```csharp
public IActionResult Filter([FromSwapState] ProductFilterState state)
{
    if (!ModelState.IsValid)
    {
        // Reset to defaults or return error
        state = new ProductFilterState();
    }
    // ...
}
```

## Summary

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `SwapState` | Base class for state objects |
| `<swap-state>` | Tag helper that renders hidden fields |
| `[FromSwapState]` | Attribute that binds state from request |
| `hx-include` | HTMX attribute that sends hidden fields with request |
| `ContainerId` | Auto-generated ID for the hidden fields container |

**The pattern:**
1. Define state class inheriting from `SwapState`
2. Render with `<swap-state state="Model.State" />`
3. Use `hx-include="#container-id"` on interactive elements
4. Bind with `[FromSwapState]` in controller
5. Return partial view with updated state

**HTML is the state. Server renders it. Browser sends it back. No JavaScript required.**
